name: Accessible Falsifier Boundary
oneSentenceClaim: A claim can be stronger when a decisive public falsifier is directly executable, even if some descriptive fields are thin; complete fields without falsifier access remain weak.
whyExistingPrinciplesInsufficient: Existing concepts identify risk sources but do not predict dominance among falsifier access, resolution cost, executable specificity, and controls.
mechanism: claim strength is capped by the accessibility of a decisive falsifier and only then adjusted by evidence-field completeness
nonExamples: broad benchmark superiority without runnable falsifier
ambiguous protocol claim with no decisive check
falsificationTests: rival predicts observation with fewer caveats
scope: safe public computational claims with retrievable artifacts
